sources.label: Power Integrations ultra-slim auxiliary PSU for NVIDIA Kyber 800VDC AI data centers (June 1, 2026 - pre-cutoff)
Simply Wall St - POWI valuation check after NVIDIA Kyber AI data center power designs
TipRanks - shareholders back board, pay and incentive plan (June 3, 2026 annual meeting - pre-cutoff)

verdicts.rankRationale: No genuinely new information since June 6; the NVIDIA Kyber 800VDC PSU designs (June 1) are already reflected in the ~97% YTD rerating. Today's -4.25% pullback with the thesis intact modestly restores asymmetry, justifying a small positive drift but no rank change after yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: PowiGaN 1700V single-HEMT auxiliary PSUs are designed into NVIDIA's Kyber 800VDC rack architecture, giving a $4.7B small-cap direct content in the AI power-delivery bottleneck; a 4% down day cheapens the entry without any thesis damage.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 revenue of $108M with thin net income shows the AI data-center segment is still small versus the legacy industrial/consumer base; the stock now trades on the AI option, not current earnings.
verdicts.bear: After ~97% YTD and a ~78% 90-day run, the name is no longer hidden; analyst fair-value chases ($56 to $85) signal post-rerating crowding, and the consumer/industrial power cycle can swamp the AI sliver.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Quantified 800VDC/Kyber design-win revenue or Q2 guidance showing data-center mix inflecting.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression as the AI-power trade de-crowds while legacy industrial/consumer power demand stays soft.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved POWI down on this page for being less direct than HBM/packaging/CPO bottleneck nodes; nothing since June 6 changes its node centrality, so no second consecutive move is warranted.
verdicts.bull: GaN auxiliary power content in 800VDC AI racks gives POWI a real, if small, node in the data-center power chain that most analog peers lack.
verdicts.neutral: Auxiliary PSU content per rack is modest relative to the main power train; POWI remains a selective, second-order AI node rather than a chokepoint.
verdicts.bear: Edge/auxiliary power is substitutable (SiC and competing GaN suppliers), so its network position is weak versus HBM, packaging and test bottlenecks the page is built around.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Expansion from auxiliary PSUs into higher-value main power-train sockets in 800VDC architectures.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Design-out or competitive GaN/SiC substitution eroding the already-thin AI node position.

verdicts.rankRationale: The CPO-specific link remains weak and the page already demoted POWI yesterday (83 to 94); no new CPO-relevant evidence since June 6, so discipline says hold rather than compound the move.
verdicts.bull: If 800VDC rack power becomes a standard companion to CPO-dense racks, POWI's auxiliary GaN content rides the same buildout.
verdicts.neutral: Data-center power exposure is real but orthogonal to co-packaged optics; POWI is a power-delivery adjacency, not a CPO supply-chain participant.
verdicts.bear: Near-doubled YTD with no CPO linkage means the residual alpha on this specific page is essentially exhausted; industrial/consumer power cycle risk dominates.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any concrete content attach to CPO-architecture racks (e.g., optics-adjacent power designs) that tightens the page linkage.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued rerating on a thesis this page does not credit, leaving rank vulnerable to further demotion if CPO names re-accelerate.
